questions.questionText: Given the universal set U = {integers from 1 to 10 inclusive} and set A = {2, 3, 5, 7}, which statement is correct?
options: A is the set of even numbers
questions.explanation: The complement of A (A') contains all elements of the universal set U that are not in A. Therefore, A' = {1, 4, 6, 8, 9, 10}.

questions.questionText: If P = {factors of 12} and Q = {factors of 18}, find P $\cap$ Q.
questions.explanation: P = {1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12} and Q = {1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18}. The intersection (P $\cap$ Q) contains elements common to both sets, which are {1, 2, 3, 6}.

questions.questionText: Let A = {letters in the word 'subtract'} and B = {letters in the word 'addition'}. Find n(A $\cup$ B).
questions.explanation: A={s,u,b,t,r,a,c}, B={a,d,i,t,o,n}. A $\cup$ B = {s,u,b,t,r,a,c,d,i,o,n}. The number of unique elements in the union is 10.

questions.questionText: In a group of 40 students, 20 play hockey, 25 play football and 8 play both. How many students play neither sport?
questions.explanation: Number playing at least one sport = n(H $\cup$ F) = n(H) + n(F) - n(H $\cap$ F) = 20 + 25 - 8 = 37. Number playing neither = Total - n(H $\cup$ F) = 40 - 37 = 3.

questions.questionText: Using the information from the previous question (40 students, 20 play hockey, 25 play football, 8 play both), how many students play football only?
questions.explanation: The number of students who play football only is the total number who play football minus those who play both sports: 25 - 8 = 17.

questions.questionText: If n(U) = 45 and n(C) = 19, what is n(C')?
questions.explanation: The number of elements in the complement of C is the total number of elements in the universal set minus the number of elements in C: n(C') = n(U) - n(C) = 45 - 19 = 26.

questions.questionText: Which statement is true if A ⊂ B?
options: Every element of B is in A
A and B have no common elements
Every element of A is also in B
questions.explanation: The symbol ⊂ means 'is a proper subset of'. This means that all elements of set A are contained within set B, and B has at least one element not in A.

questions.questionText: Let U = {x | x is an integer and 1 $\leq$ x $\leq$ 20}. A = {multiples of 2}, B = {multiples of 5}. Find n(A $\cap$ B).
questions.explanation: The intersection A $\cap$ B contains numbers that are multiples of both 2 and 5, which are multiples of 10. In the given range, these are {10, 20}. Therefore, n(A $\cap$ B) = 2.

questions.questionText: The Venn diagram shows two sets, P and Q. What does the shaded region represent?
questions.explanation: The shaded region is outside both circles P and Q. This represents the elements that are not in P and not in Q, which is the complement of the union of P and Q, written as (P $\cup$ Q)'.

questions.questionText: Given P = {odd numbers} and Q = {prime numbers}, which number is in P $\cap$ Q'?
questions.explanation: We need an odd number (P) that is NOT a prime number (Q'). From the options, 9 is an odd number but it is not prime (9 = 3x3). 7 and 11 are prime. 2 is not odd.

questions.questionText: Set A has 5 elements and Set B has 8 elements. If n(A $\cap$ B) = 3, what is n(A $\cup$ B)?
questions.explanation: Using the formula n(A $\cup$ B) = n(A) + n(B) - n(A $\cap$ B), we get n(A $\cup$ B) = 5 + 8 - 3 = 10.

questions.questionText: Which of the following pairs of sets are disjoint?
questions.explanation: Disjoint sets have no elements in common (their intersection is the empty set). A number cannot be both even and odd, so these sets are disjoint.
